title: Fix 'Actor not available' error on client creation

requirements.summary: Diagnose and fix the 'Actor not available' error on the New Client page by ensuring the actor is initialized before mutations and showing a loading state instead of an error banner when the actor is not yet ready
acceptanceCriteria: When a fully authenticated user navigates to /clients/new, no 'Actor not available' error banner is displayed.
The Create button is not disabled while the actor is initializing — the page shows a spinner or loading indicator instead.
Once the actor is ready, pressing Create with valid field values calls createClient on the backend actor and navigates back to /clients on success.
If the actor genuinely fails to initialize, a user-friendly error message is shown (not a raw 'Actor not available' string).

file_operations.description: Guard the createClient mutation call so it only fires when the actor is confirmed non-null. Replace the current error banner that shows 'Actor not available' with a loading indicator when the actor is initializing. Use the isFetching or isLoading state from useActor to determine when the actor is still being created, and show a spinner or 'Connecting...' message in place of the error banner. Once the actor is ready, allow the Create button to trigger the mutation. If the actor fails to initialize (error state), display a user-friendly error message such as 'Unable to connect. Please try again.' instead of the raw 'Actor not available' text. Ensure the createClient mutation receives the actor reference correctly and does not fall back to a no-op when the actor is undefined.

requirements.summary: Ensure useActor creates the actor as soon as an authenticated identity is available, caches it via React Query, and returns a loading state (not an error) during initialization
acceptanceCriteria: useActor returns a stable actor reference for the same identity without reinitialization on each render.
While the actor is being created, useActor returns { isLoading: true, actor: undefined } — no error is thrown.
After the actor is ready, isLoading is false and actor is the authenticated backend actor instance.
The actor instance is invalidated and recreated only when the identity changes (login/logout).

file_operations.description: Review and adjust all mutation hooks (useCreateClient, useUpdateClient, useDeleteClient, useCreateJob, etc.) to ensure they do not throw 'Actor not available' errors when the actor is undefined. Instead, each mutation hook should check if the actor is available before calling mutationFn. If the actor is not ready, the mutation should either wait (by deferring the call) or gracefully handle the absence by returning a pending state. Ensure that mutations are wired to receive the actor reference from useActor and that they do not fall back to a no-op or throw an error when the actor is undefined during initialization. Verify that the enabled or retry logic does not cause premature error states.

requirements.summary: Guard the createClient mutation in ClientDetailPage so it only fires when the actor is confirmed non-null, and show a loading indicator on the button instead of propagating an error
acceptanceCriteria: The Create button in new-client mode is never disabled due to actor unavailability after the user is authenticated.
Pressing Create when the actor is not yet ready shows a loading spinner on the button and retries once the actor is available, or displays a clear 'Please wait, connecting…' message.
When the actor is ready and the form has valid values, pressing Create calls actor.createClient with the correct argument shape and navigates to /clients on success.
Any error returned by the backend is surfaced as a visible error message in the form.

file_operations.description: Add logic to disable or show a loading spinner on the Create button when the actor is not yet available. Use the isFetching or isLoading state from useActor to determine if the actor is still initializing. While the actor is loading, display a spinner on the button and/or a 'Please wait, connecting…' message instead of allowing the user to press Create and encounter an error. Once the actor is ready, enable the button and allow the mutation to proceed. Ensure that the createClient mutation is only called when actor is non-null. If the mutation fails due to a backend error, display the error message inline in the form (e.g., in a toast or error banner below the Create button).
